title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Eval Recall
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps.
Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.
Specify output structure, granularity, evidence, and machine-readable constraints when they matter.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio

steps.title: Goal First · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Goal First without dropping earlier contracts?

content.text: State the desired result and audience before prescribing implementation steps. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Goal First, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A prompt should begin with every action the model must take. This fails the Goal First contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

stimulus.title: Done Conditions case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt prompt anatomy. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Done means the model produced a fluent answer.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting. Artifact under review: a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Done Conditions, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Done Conditions applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define observable completion checks including tests, review, and unresolved-evidence reporting.
